proposed_features.feature: presence of personal experience or narrative
proposed_features.description: One group includes texts that describe personal actions, feelings, or reflections, while the other group avoids personal perspective and focuses on objective or impersonal content.
Some texts provide step-by-step instructions or procedures to follow, distinguishing them from texts that do not contain direct instructions.

proposed_features.feature: use of technical or domain-specific terminology
proposed_features.description: Texts that include specialized vocabulary related to technical, scientific, or professional domains can be separated from those that use everyday language.

proposed_features.feature: presence of assumptions or theoretical framing
proposed_features.description: Some texts explicitly state assumptions or theoretical perspectives, which can differentiate analytical or academic style from narrative or practical texts.
Texts that describe how to resolve an issue or fix a problem contrast with those that do not address problem-solving directly.
Presence of words expressing feelings, expectations, or subjective reactions can separate personal or narrative texts from neutral or objective ones.

proposed_features.feature: description of experimental or controlled conditions
proposed_features.description: Texts that describe controlled environments or experimental setups differ from those that do not mention such conditions.

proposed_features.feature: temporal framing or sequence of events
proposed_features.description: Some texts narrate events over time or describe changes and progressions, while others focus on static information or instructions.
Texts that use commands or direct requests (imperative verbs) can be distinguished from those that use declarative or descriptive sentences.
Some texts discuss abstract concepts or general principles, while others focus on concrete actions or specific events.
